content.problemStatement:
There are NNN stones in a pond, each having a value AiA_iAi‚Äč written on it. A frog is at stone 111 and wants to reach stone NNN. The frog can jump from a stone iii to any stone j(j>i)j (j>i)j(j>i). Let ddd be the length of subarray (i.e. j‚ąíi+1j-i+1j‚ąíi+1), then the energy required for the jump is (d‚čÖAi)‚ąíAj(d \cdot A_i)-A_j(d‚čÖAi‚Äč)‚ąíAj‚Äč. Find the minimum **non-negative** amount of energy required by the frog to reach the NNN-th stone.

**Note**: It is possible that the **total** amount of energy required is negative, in that case, you should print the minimum non-negative value (i.e. 000).


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the number of stones.
The second line contains NNN integers denoting the numbers written on the stones.


Output Format
For each test case output a single integer - the minimum **non-negative** energy required by the frog.
constraints: 1‚Č§T‚Č§25001 \le T \le 25001‚Č§T‚Č§2500 - the number of test cases
1‚Č§Ai‚Č§1091 \le A_i \le 10^91‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§109 - the number written on stones.
Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055 \cdot 10^{5} 5‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: The frog can jump to the 2nd2^{nd}2nd stone from the 1st1^{st}1st stone, and then to the 3rd3^{rd}3rd stone from the 2nd2^{nd}2nd stone.
Test Case 333: The frog can jump to the 3rd3^{rd}3rd stone directly from the 1st1^{st}1st stone.
Test Case 444: The frog can jump to the 2nd2^{nd}2nd stone from the 1st1^{st}1st stone. However, the energy required in this case is negative (i.e. ‚ąí3-3‚ąí3), So we will print 000.
